ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet anger mottagaren som 'General Food Processing Industries' i Dubai, Förenade Arabemiraten, men fakturan är ställd till 'Agro Food Solutions' i Haag, Nederländerna, och leveransadressen anger 'Kadbanoo Co.' i Tehran, Iran. Mottagaren i certifikatet kan inte identifieras i fakturan. Dessutom kan certifikatets kvantitet (8820 kg net) inte verifieras mot fakturans uppgifter (17 640 kg net / 18 346 kg brutto).
overall_assessment.summary: Verification failed on consignee (certificate states General Food Processing Industries in UAE; invoice is addressed to Agro Food Solutions in Netherlands with ship-to Kadbanoo Co. in Iran — certificate consignee not found in invoice) and quantity (certificate 8820 kg net vs invoice 17640 kg net). Consignor and origin verified successfully.

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Good quality, 3 pages all identical content (ORIGINAL 1/3, 2/3, 3/3). Clear text extraction.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Good quality, single page. Clear text. Contains Källbergs branding/logo at top right.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number 'SEG-25D-376208' does not contain an LC bank-term reference.

locations.location: Footer — issuer address block: 'Källbergs Industri AB, Skövdevägen 25, Box 73, 54522 Töreboda, Sverige'

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Certificate consignor 'Källbergs Industri AB' identified in invoice footer as 'Källbergs Industri AB' and in branding as 'KÄLLBERGS'.
pairing_validation.motivation: Pairing validation passed: the certificate's consignor (Källbergs Industri AB) is explicitly identified as the invoice issuer in the footer and branding of the invoice. This confirms the documents relate to the same transaction.

candidates.normalized_text: p.o. box 81931, dubai, united arab emirates

General Food Processing Industries L.L.C.

candidates.normalized_text: general food processing industries l.l.c., united arabemirates

candidates.raw_text: Louise Henriettestraat 3, NL-2595TN The Hauge, The Netherlands
candidates.normalized_text: louise henriettestraat 3, nl-2595tn the hague, the netherlands

candidates.raw_text: No. 24, 7t Fath St, Fath highway, Tehran, Iran
candidates.normalized_text: no. 24, 7t fath st, fath highway, tehran, iran

checkpoints.certificate_value: Källbergs Industri AB, Box 73, SE-545 22, TÖREBODA, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Källbergs Industri AB, Skövdevägen 25, Box 73, 54522 Töreboda, Sverige

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'Källbergs Industri AB' in all invoice pages.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND — consignor name 'Källbergs Industri AB' found in invoice footer issuer block and in branding ('KÄLLBERGS').

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignor name with invoice issuer name.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: 'Källbergs Industri AB' — Invoice footer: 'Källbergs Industri AB'. Exact match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Compare consignor country.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: 'Sweden'. Invoice footer: 'Sverige' (Swedish for Sweden). Also VAT prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur — consignor name found in invoice footer/issuer block
Country verified via VAT prefix SE and 'Sverige' = Sweden
Country check — Sweden = Sverige after normalization

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Källbergs Industri AB' in Sweden. The invoice is issued by 'Källbergs Industri AB' as shown in the footer block (Källbergs Industri AB, Skövdevägen 25, Box 73, 54522 Töreboda, Sverige) and the KÄLLBERGS branding at the top of the invoice. Name matches exactly. Country: certificate says 'Sweden', invoice footer says 'Sverige' (Swedish for Sweden), and VAT number prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden. Both name and country verified. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Consignor name 'Källbergs Industri AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice as the issuer (footer block). Country verified: 'Sweden' in certificate matches 'Sverige' in invoice, confirmed by VAT prefix SE.

checkpoints.certificate_value: General Food Processing Industries, P.O. Box 81931, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
checkpoints.invoice_value: Buyer: Agro Food Solutions, Louise Henriettestraat 3, NL-2595TN The Hauge, The Netherlands | Ship-to: Kadbanoo Co., Tehran, Iran

comparison_steps.description: Identify the primary billing party in the invoice. The invoice lists 'Agro Food Solutions' in the buyer/bill-to block (top left, below invoice header).
comparison_steps.result: Primary billing party: 'Agro Food Solutions', The Netherlands. Certificate consignee: 'General Food Processing Industries', UAE. Names are completely different — no match.

comparison_steps.description: Check Ship-to Address field. Invoice lists 'Kadbanoo Co.' in Tehran, Iran.
comparison_steps.result: Ship-to party: 'Kadbanoo Co.', Iran. Certificate consignee: 'General Food Processing Industries', UAE. Names are completely different — no match. Country also differs (Iran vs UAE).

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.1 – Additional search in all invoice fields
comparison_steps.description: Search for 'General Food Processing Industries' anywhere in the invoice text.
comparison_steps.result: NOT FOUND. The string 'General Food Processing Industries' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. No partial match found either.

comparison_steps.description: The names 'General Food Processing Industries' and 'Agro Food Solutions' share the word 'Food' but are otherwise completely different legal entities. This is not a normalization case.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH — different legal entities.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification — primary billing field 'Agro Food Solutions' does not match certificate consignee
Additional search — 'General Food Processing Industries' not found anywhere in invoice
Legal party check — different company names, not a normalization issue

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'General Food Processing Industries' in Dubai, UAE. The invoice's primary billing party (buyer/bill-to) is 'Agro Food Solutions' in The Netherlands — a completely different company name and country. The ship-to party is 'Kadbanoo Co.' in Tehran, Iran — also a completely different name and country (Iran vs UAE). A full-text search for 'General Food Processing Industries' in the invoice yields zero results. The certificate consignee cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. Per rule 4.2.0.2, when a primary billing field exists with a named commercial party, the consignee must be verifiable against that party. The Ship-To rule (4.2.0.3) also does not help because the ship-to party 'Kadbanoo Co.' does not match the certificate consignee 'General Food Processing Industries'. MISMATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate consignee 'General Food Processing Industries' (Dubai, UAE) cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. The invoice's buyer is 'Agro Food Solutions' (The Netherlands) and the ship-to is 'Kadbanoo Co.' (Tehran, Iran). Neither matches the certificate's consignee by name or country. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Could 'General Food Processing Industries' and 'Agro Food Solutions' be related companies?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.1.3.1, the system may not assume konzern relations, agent relations, or trade relationships. The names are completely different legal entities. Only the word 'Food' is shared, which is a generic industry term. No further connection can be established from the documents.

The Ship-To exception requires that the certificate's consignee can be identified exactly in the invoice's Ship-To field. The Ship-To is 'Kadbanoo Co.' in Iran, which does not match 'General Food Processing Industries' in UAE.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate does not contain an explicit article number. Invoice contains item no. '1541119'. No article number in certificate to compare.
comparison_steps.result: Not applicable — no article number in certificate.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399'. Invoice: 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)'. Identify the identity-bearing main designation.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate main designation: 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399'. Invoice: 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)'. The terms 'Heat Stable EYP' vs 'Food Shaping Compound' are completely different words — not a normalization case. However, 'Mayo-Max399' in the certificate and 'Mayomax' in the invoice share the substring 'Mayo' and 'Max'.

comparison_steps.description: Check if all words from certificate's main designation appear in invoice in same order.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate words: 'Heat', 'Stable', 'EYP', 'Mayo-Max399'. Invoice words: 'Food', 'Shaping', 'Compound', 'Mayomax'. The words 'Heat', 'Stable', 'EYP' do NOT appear in the invoice at all. Word sequence matching fails.

comparison_steps.description: The certificate uses 'Mayo-Max399' which could be a product code/name, and the invoice uses 'Mayomax' in parentheses which could be the same product line abbreviated differently. This requires industry knowledge to establish — the relationship between 'Mayo-Max399' and '(Mayomax)' is suggestive but not a direct textual match.
comparison_steps.result: MANUAL_REVIEW — the designations suggest the same product family but require interpretation beyond permitted rules. 'Mayo-Max399' contains 'Mayo' and 'Max' which appear concatenated as 'Mayomax' in the invoice, but this is a semantic interpretation.

rules_applied.description: Identity-bearing main designation — 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399' cannot be identified in invoice text 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)'
Short designation rule — 'Mayomax' in invoice could be a short form of 'Mayo-Max399' but requires interpretation

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399'. Invoice goods: 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)'. The primary product descriptions use completely different terms: 'Heat Stable EYP' vs 'Food Shaping Compound'. These are not normalizable variations — they are different words. However, the product name component 'Mayo-Max399' (certificate) and 'Mayomax' (invoice, in parentheses) share a clear relationship: removing the hyphen and '399' from 'Mayo-Max399' yields something close to 'MayoMax' ≈ 'Mayomax'. This suggests the same product but establishing equivalence requires semantic interpretation beyond permitted rules. Under rule 4.3.3.3 (short designation), the invoice's 'Mayomax' could be a short form for the certificate's product, occurring in the same product context. This triggers MANUAL_REVIEW rather than MISMATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: The certificate's product description 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399' and the invoice's 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)' use entirely different terminology for the product category, but share the product name element 'Mayo-Max' / 'Mayomax'. Establishing equivalence requires interpretation beyond permitted textual matching. Per rule 4.3.3.3, this warrants MANUAL_REVIEW.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Could 'Mayo-Max399' and 'Mayomax' be treated as exact match after normalization?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: 'Mayo-Max399' contains a version/lot number '399' and a hyphen. Removing these to get 'MayoMax' and then comparing to 'Mayomax' requires semantic interpretation (removing numeric suffix, normalizing case). This goes beyond permitted normalization (versaler/gemener, mellanslag, bindestreck). The '399' is part of the identity-bearing designation.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because descriptions are completely different?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: While the main descriptive terms differ ('Heat Stable EYP' vs 'Food Shaping Compound'), the shared product name element 'Mayo-Max' / 'Mayomax' in the same product context suggests the same product family. Rule 4.3.3.3 explicitly covers this scenario (short designation / code where the relationship requires industry knowledge) and directs to MANUAL_REVIEW rather than MISMATCH.

checkpoints.invoice_value: Total Net Weight 17.640 (= 17640 kg after thousands separator normalization)

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states '8820 kg net' in Box 7. Numeric value: 8820. Unit: kg. Weight category: net. All required elements present.
comparison_steps.result: Valid quantity specification in certificate.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains '8820 kg net'. This is the verification-basis quantity.
comparison_steps.result: Verification basis: 8820 kg net.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 8820 in invoice. Invoice contains: Qty: 17.640 Kg, Total Net Weight: 17.640, Total Gross Weight: 18.346. The value '8820' does not appear in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: 8820 NOT FOUND in invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice shows '17.640' for quantity and Total Net Weight. Considering '17.640' as '17640' (with period as thousands separator — value is listed as 'Kg' quantity on the item line with unit price 9.55 and total 168,462.00; 17640 × 9.55 = 168,462.00 confirming 17640 kg). Certificate says 8820 kg net. 8820 ≠ 17640.
comparison_steps.result: Even after thousands separator normalization, 8820 ≠ 17640. MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Check exceptions 3A through 3E.
comparison_steps.result: 3A: Not applicable — single invoice. 3B: MT↔KG conversion — certificate 8820 kg = 8.82 MT. Invoice 17640 kg = 17.64 MT. 8.82 ≠ 17.64. No match. 3C: Not applicable — not a per-piece weight scenario. 3D: Invoice has only one product line (17640 kg). No multi-row summation possible to get 8820. 3E: No row-level weights to sum. No exception applies.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate Net Weight: 8820 kg. Invoice Total Net Weight: 17640 kg (after normalization). Both specify net weight. Values differ: 8820 vs 17640. This is a 100% difference — far exceeds ±0.1% tolerance. Explicit contradiction.
comparison_steps.result: EXPLICIT CONTRADICTION per rule 4.4.3.1 — both documents state net weight, values are 8820 kg vs 17640 kg.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value '8820 kg net' used as verification basis
Main rule — 8820 not found in invoice
Thousands separator normalization applied — invoice '17.640' = 17640 kg
Explicit contradiction — both documents state net weight but values differ (8820 vs 17640)

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate Box 7: '8820 kg net'. Invoice: 'Total Net Weight 17.640' (which is 17640 kg, confirmed by line item qty 17.640 Kg × 9.55 = 168,462.00). Certificate net weight 8820 kg vs invoice net weight 17640 kg. The value 8820 is exactly half of 17640, suggesting the certificate may cover only part of the invoice's goods (e.g., only certain lots), but no rule permits halving or partial quantity acceptance. Both documents explicitly state a net weight, and the values do not match within the ±0.1% tolerance. This is an explicit contradiction per rule 4.4.3.1. Note: the certificate mentions '126 bags of lot 25-12818' and '462 bags of lot 25-12873' totaling 588 bags. At 15 kg/bag this would be 8820 kg, which aligns with the certificate's quantity. The invoice shows the full 17640 kg order. The certificate appears to cover only a portion. Regardless, the numerical values explicitly contradict each other. MISMATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate states 8820 kg net. Invoice states Total Net Weight 17640 kg. Both are net weight values and they differ by 100% (8820 vs 17640). This is an explicit contradiction exceeding the ±0.1% tolerance. No exception (3A–3E) is applicable. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Could 8820 be a partial shipment quantity that should be accepted?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: No rule permits accepting a certificate quantity that is exactly half of the invoice quantity. The certificate covers 588 bags (126 + 462) at presumably 15 kg/bag = 8820 kg, but the invoice total is 17640 kg. There is no partial-shipment tolerance rule. The explicit net weight values contradict each other.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Could the '17.640' in the invoice be 17.64 kg (with decimal point)?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: This interpretation is excluded by context: unit price 9.55 EUR/kg × 17.64 kg = 168.47 EUR, but invoice total is 168,462.00 EUR. Only 17640 kg × 9.55 = 168,462.00 EUR is consistent. The period is a thousands separator.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Should MANUAL_REVIEW apply per 4.4.6.1 (invoice lacks quantity)?
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.4.6.1 applies only when the invoice COMPLETELY LACKS a quantity/weight. Here the invoice explicitly states Total Net Weight 17640 — the invoice does have a quantity, it just doesn't match. MISMATCH, not MANUAL_REVIEW.

comparison_steps.description: Search for origin declaration in invoice. Found: 'Country fo origin: Sweden' (note: 'fo' is likely a typo for 'of'). This is an explicit origin field.
comparison_steps.result: Origin field found: Sweden.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'EC: Sweden' = European Community: Sweden. Invoice: 'Sweden'. Compare 'Sweden' ↔ 'Sweden'.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH — Sweden = Sweden.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate uses 'EC' (European Community) prefix. Sweden is an EU member state. Invoice confirms 'Sweden' as origin. This is consistent.
comparison_steps.result: EC/EU normalization confirms Sweden is a valid EU member. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Check if invoice contains any non-EU origin countries. Invoice only mentions 'Sweden' as origin. No non-EU origins found.
comparison_steps.result: No contradicting non-EU origins in invoice.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'Country fo origin: Sweden' field in invoice
EU normalization — EC: Sweden consistent with Sweden as EU member

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'EC: Sweden' (European Community: Sweden). Invoice origin: 'Country fo origin: Sweden' (with typo 'fo' for 'of'). The origin country 'Sweden' is explicitly stated in both documents. Sweden is an EU member state, consistent with the 'EC' prefix. No conflicting origins in the invoice. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate states origin 'EC: Sweden'. Invoice explicitly states 'Country fo origin: Sweden'. Sweden matches Sweden. No contradicting origins found. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in certificate.
Document A is the certificate (3-page EU Certificate of Origin). Document B is the invoice (Invoice SI02108 by Källbergs Industri AB). Single certificate, single invoice — straightforward pairing.
Criterion 2 met: Certificate consignor 'Källbergs Industri AB' found in invoice as issuer. Pairing validated.
Consignor 'Källbergs Industri AB' found in invoice. Country Sweden verified.

Certificate consignee 'General Food Processing Industries' (UAE) not found anywhere in invoice. Invoice buyer is 'Agro Food Solutions' (Netherlands), ship-to is 'Kadbanoo Co.' (Iran). Complete name and country mismatch.

Product descriptions differ: 'Heat Stable EYP Mayo-Max399' vs 'Food Shaping Compound (Mayomax)'. Shared element 'Mayo-Max'/'Mayomax' suggests same product but requires interpretation. Rule 4.3.3.3 applied.
Certificate 8820 kg net vs invoice 17640 kg net. Explicit contradiction — values differ by 100%.
Certificate 'EC: Sweden', invoice 'Country fo origin: Sweden'. Both state Sweden. MATCH.

human_readable_report.title: Verification Report — Certificate of Origin vs Invoice
human_readable_report.result_summary: NOT_IDENTICAL — Consignee mismatch and quantity mismatch identified.

sections.certificate_value: Källbergs Industri AB, Box 73, SE-545 22, TÖREBODA, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Källbergs Industri AB, Skövdevägen 25, Box 73, 54522 Töreboda, Sverige (issuer in footer)
sections.rule_applied: Consignor name exactly matches invoice issuer. Country Sweden verified via 'Sverige' and VAT prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Exact name match. Country confirmed through multiple indicators (address 'Sverige', VAT prefix 'SE'). MATCH.

sections.certificate_value: General Food Processing Industries, P.O. Box 81931, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
sections.invoice_value: Buyer: Agro Food Solutions, The Netherlands. Ship-to: Kadbanoo Co., Tehran, Iran.
sections.rule_applied: Certificate consignee 'General Food Processing Industries' not found in any field of the invoice. Invoice buyer and ship-to are completely different entities.
sections.motivation: Certificate states consignee as 'General Food Processing Industries' in Dubai, UAE. Invoice buyer is 'Agro Food Solutions' (Netherlands) and ship-to is 'Kadbanoo Co.' (Iran). The certificate's consignee name cannot be found anywhere in the invoice text. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Product descriptions use different terms. Shared product name element 'Mayo-Max' / 'Mayomax' suggests same product family but establishing equivalence requires interpretation.
sections.motivation: The identity-bearing main designations differ significantly ('Heat Stable EYP' vs 'Food Shaping Compound'). The product name element 'Mayo-Max399' (cert) and 'Mayomax' (invoice) suggest the same product but require industry knowledge to confirm. MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 4.3.3.3.

sections.rule_applied: Both documents state net weight. Certificate 8820 kg vs invoice 17640 kg. Explicit contradiction exceeding ±0.1% tolerance.
sections.motivation: Certificate net weight 8820 kg does not match invoice net weight 17640 kg. Both documents explicitly state net weight. The difference of 8820 kg (100%) far exceeds the ±0.1% tolerance. No applicable exception. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Sweden in certificate matches Sweden in invoice. EU membership confirmed for Sweden.
sections.motivation: Certificate states 'EC: Sweden'. Invoice states 'Country fo origin: Sweden'. Origin country Sweden is identical. MATCH.

output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_check: N/A — comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL, confidence floor only applies to IDENTICAL
output_quality_checks.notes: Two checkpoints (consignee and quantity) have MISMATCH status. One checkpoint (goods description) has MANUAL_REVIEW. Consignor and origin are MATCH. Overall result NOT_IDENTICAL is mandated by the presence of MISMATCH checkpoints.
